generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor for monitoring indoor environmental conditions via Eclipse Hono for real-time data acquisition."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Humidity sensor for monitoring indoor environmental conditions via Eclipse Hono for real-time data acquisition."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalHumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy sensor for detecting presence of users in building spaces via Eclipse Hono for real-time data acquisition."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Light level sensor for monitoring ambient lighting conditions via Eclipse Hono for real-time data acquisition."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Power meter for tracking real-time energy consumption via Eclipse Hono for data acquisition."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance VisualComfortSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy sensor for collecting visual comfort data as part of BOREALIS project for reinforcement learning optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VisualComfortDataTransmitted
]

instance NonVisualHealthEffectSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy sensor for collecting non-visual health effect data as part of BOREALIS project for reinforcement learning optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData NonVisualHealthEffectDataTransmitted
]

instance UserBehaviorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy sensor for collecting user behavior data as part of BOREALIS project for reinforcement learning optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData UserBehaviorDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalHumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance LightLevelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionMeter
]

instance VisualComfortDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VisualComfortSensor
]

instance NonVisualHealthEffectDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom NonVisualHealthEffectSensor
]

instance UserBehaviorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom UserBehaviorSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimEnrichedSemanticModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM model incorporating geometric, spatial, functional attributes and runtime semantics (sensor/actuator configurations, control logic) as foundational data schema."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler, ModelDrivenTransformationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data from IoT sensors and Building Control Systems ingested via Eclipse Hono and stored in Ditto for state management."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalHumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted,
                  LightLevelDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, VisualComfortDataTransmitted,
                  NonVisualHealthEffectDataTransmitted, UserBehaviorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance ReinforcementLearningControlPolicy : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning policies (as in BOREALIS) embedded within the DT to automate adaptive control decisions based on post-occupancy data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReinforcementLearningFrameworkEnabler
]

instance RuleBasedControlStrategy : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Rule-based control strategies (as in TwinLight) for automated decision-making in building systems."
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D visualization model rendered via Unreal Engine 5 for immersive monitoring and interactive control of building systems."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngine5Enabler
]

instance CustomServiceExtensionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Modular extension models for domain-specific services (e.g., predictive maintenance, adaptive control algorithms) integrated via standardized extension points."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ServiceIntegrationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight IoT messaging framework facilitating real-time bidirectional communication between physical sensors/actuators and the DT."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, AutomatedAlertService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin state management platform providing standardized interfaces for data exchange and service integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, SimulationAndOptimizationService, CustomServiceIntegrationService
]

instance ModelDrivenTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-to-model and model-to-text transformation framework automating runtime artifact generation from BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:enables TwinningProcessService, AutomatedDeploymentService
]

instance UnrealEngine5Enabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization engine providing immersive monitoring and interactive control interfaces for the building twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance ReinforcementLearningFrameworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning framework (as in BOREALIS) enabling adaptive control policies that evolve based on post-occupancy data."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance ControlLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Control logic framework embedding rule-based strategies for automated building system management."
]

instance ServiceIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular architecture framework supporting standardized extension points for custom service integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomServiceIntegrationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard service displaying live sensor data, system states, and performance metrics via Unreal Engine 5 visualizations."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeBuildingStateInsight, EnergyWasteInsight, OccupancyPatternInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing what-if analyses and optimization suggestions for building systems based on real-time data and predictive models."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationInsight, VisualComfortOptimizationInsight, HealthEffectOptimizationInsight,
                  LightingAdjustmentAction, HVACSetpointAdjustmentAction
]

instance SimulationAndOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting what-if scenarios and life cycle optimization by simulating operational changes and evaluating impacts."
    DTDFVocab:provides RefurbishmentImpactInsight, EnergyEfficiencyScenarioInsight, UserExperienceImpactInsight
]

instance AutomatedAlertService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service triggering alerts for anomalies such as equipment failures or energy spikes to enable proactive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:provides EquipmentFailureAlertAction, EnergySpikeAlertAction
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling integration of domain-specific services (e.g., predictive maintenance, adaptive control algorithms) via standardized extension points."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing immersive 3D visualizations of the building for intuitive monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:provides Interactive3DBuildingViewInsight
]

instance TwinningProcessService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing model enrichment, validation, and automated deployment of digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelEnrichmentInsight, DeploymentValidationInsight
]

instance AutomatedDeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service scanning BIM models to extract metadata and generate runtime interfaces for real-time connectivity."
    DTDFVocab:provides RuntimeInterfaceGenerationInsight
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

]

]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting system actuator executing commands from the DT to adjust light intensity based on occupancy and visual comfort data."
]

instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC control actuator executing commands from the DT to adjust temperature and airflow based on environmental conditions."
]

instance ShadingMechanismActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Shading mechanism actuator executing commands from the DT to adjust blinds based on light levels and user preferences."
]

instance BuildingAutomationSystemController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building automation system controller processing inputs and generating control signals for actuators based on reinforcement learning policies."
]

instance ReinforcementLearningController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning-based controller (as in BOREALIS) generating adaptive control policies for building systems."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world are enabled through **bidirectional communication protocols** and **automated control logic**. Key aspects include:
  - **Control Signal Generation**: The DT generates control signals (e.g., lighting adjustments, HVAC setpoints) based on real-time data analysis, predictive models, or user-defined policies (e.g., reinforcement learning in BOREALIS).
  - **Actuation via Middleware**: Control signals are transmitted to physical actuators (e.g., lighting systems, dampers) through **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto**, which serve as intermediaries for real-time command execution.
  - **Automated Commissioning**: The framework supports **automated deployment of control logic** directly from BIM models, enabling seamless integration of virtual control strategies with physical Building Control Systems (BCS).
  - **Feedback Loops**: The DT continuously validates the effectiveness of control actions by comparing virtual predictions with physical outcomes, ensuring alignment between the digital and physical systems."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time** for operational synchronization and **event-driven** for dynamic updates. Key aspects include:
  - **Real-Time Synchronization**: The DT maintains continuous alignment with the physical twin through **low-latency data exchange** (via Eclipse Hono and Ditto), ensuring up-to-date representations of sensor data and system states.
  - **Event-Driven Updates**: The framework processes **asynchronous events** (e.g., sensor triggers, user interactions) to update the DT dynamically, enabling responsive control and monitoring.
  - **Batch Processing for Analytics**: While real-time is prioritized for operational control, **batch processing** is used for long-term analytics (e.g., energy consumption trends) and reinforcement learning model training (as in BOREALIS)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented architecture** with the following components:
  - **Core DT Framework**: Acts as the **central hub** for bidirectional data exchange and semantic integration, leveraging BIM as the unified model backbone.
  - **Middleware Layer**: **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** provide the **connectivity infrastructure** for real-time data ingestion and command execution.
  - **Visualization Layer**: **Unreal Engine 5** delivers **interactive 3D visualizations** of the building twin, synchronized with real-time data.
  - **Service Layer**: Modular services (e.g., energy optimization, predictive maintenance) are integrated via **standardized extension points**, enabling **plug-and-play extensibility**.
  - **Control Layer**: Embedded **reinforcement learning** and **rule-based controllers** (from TwinLight and BOREALIS) execute automated control strategies based on real-time data and user-defined policies."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT operates across the **entire building life cycle**, covering the following stages:
  - **Design Phase**: The DT is initialized from **BIM models** enriched with device metadata (e.g., sensor/actuator configurations) to create a **virtual representation of the as-designed building**.
  - **Construction Phase**: The DT is updated to reflect **as-manufactured** deviations (e.g., material substitutions, spatial adjustments) via model-driven transformations.
  - **Operation Phase**: The DT transitions to **as-operated** mode, where it continuously synchronizes with real-time data from sensors and actuators, enabling **predictive maintenance, energy optimization, and adaptive control**.
  - **Refurbishment/Adaptation**: The DT supports **dynamic evolution** to accommodate changes (e.g., retrofits, reconfigurations) by updating its semantic model and runtime interfaces without full redeployment."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Semantic Alignment**: The DT maintains **high fidelity** by leveraging BIM’s **semantic richness** to represent physical assets accurately, including geometric, spatial, and functional attributes.
  - **Real-Time Synchronization**: **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** ensure **low-latency data exchange**, minimizing discrepancies between the physical and virtual systems.
  - **Validation Mechanisms**: The framework includes **automated validation** of BIM models before deployment to ensure completeness and consistency.
  - **ODTE Metric**: The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE)** metric quantifies the **degree of alignment** between the DT and its physical counterpart, providing a measurable indicator of fidelity.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: The DT incorporates **adaptive control policies** (e.g., reinforcement learning) to mitigate uncertainties in sensor data or operational conditions, ensuring robust performance."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and the digital twin (DT) is established via:
  - **Network Protocols**: **MQTT** (via Eclipse Hono) is used for lightweight, efficient IoT messaging between sensors/actuators and the DT.
  - **Digital Twin State Management**: **Eclipse Ditto** provides **standardized interfaces** for managing DT instances, including **CRUD operations** on twin states and **event-driven updates**.
  - **Bidirectional Communication**: The framework supports **real-time command-and-control** via **HTTP/REST APIs** and **WebSocket connections**, enabling seamless interaction between the DT and physical systems.
  - **Middleware Orchestration**: Eclipse Hono acts as a **message broker** to aggregate and route data between heterogeneous IoT devices and the DT, while Ditto ensures **consistent state representation** across the system."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed using a **modular, cloud-native architecture** with the following components:
  - **Cloud Infrastructure**: The framework is designed for **scalable deployment** on cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure), supporting distributed DT instances for large-scale buildings.
  - **Containerization**: Services and middleware (e.g., Eclipse Hono, Ditto) are deployed as **Docker containers**, enabling **portable and isolated execution environments**.
  - **On-Premise Deployment**: The DT can also be deployed **on-premise** for facilities with strict data residency requirements, leveraging **edge computing** for low-latency processing.
  - **Unreal Engine 5 Integration**: The visualization layer is deployed as a **standalone application** or integrated into **web-based dashboards** for remote access by facility managers."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is achieved through:
  - **Standardized Interfaces**: The DT framework provides **RESTful APIs** and **event-driven hooks** for integrating with external systems (e.g., ERP, CMMS, third-party IoT platforms).
  - **BIM Interoperability**: The use of **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** and **BCF (BIM Collaboration Format)** ensures compatibility with other BIM-based tools and workflows.
  - **Middleware Agnosticism**: Eclipse Hono and Ditto support **protocol-agnostic connectivity**, allowing integration with diverse IoT ecosystems (e.g., Zigbee, LoRaWAN) and enterprise systems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards and frameworks:
  - **BIM Standards**: Leverages **Industry Foundation Classes (IFC)** and **BuildingSMART Data Dictionary (bSDD)** for semantic interoperability.
  - **IoT Standards**: Uses **MQTT** (via Eclipse Hono) for IoT messaging and **OPC UA** for industrial interoperability where applicable.
  - **Digital Twin Standards**: Aligns with **ISO/IEC 23247** (Digital Twin Framework) and **CEN/TC 448** (Digital Twin for Buildings) for architectural and operational consistency.
  - **Middleware Standards**: Eclipse Hono and Ditto comply with **open-source IoT and digital twin standards**, ensuring vendor-neutral integration."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Authentication and Authorization**: Eclipse Hono and Ditto implement **OAuth 2.0** and **JWT-based authentication** to secure data exchange between the PT and DT.
  - **Data Encryption**: **TLS/SSL** is used for encrypting data in transit, while **role-based access control (RBAC)** restricts unauthorized modifications to the DT or physical systems.
  - **Safety Protocols**: The framework includes **fail-safe mechanisms** to prevent unintended control actions (e.g., emergency shutdowns) and **audit logs** for tracking system changes.
  - **Compliance**: The DT design aligns with **ISO/IEC 27001** (Information Security) and **ISO 55000** (Asset Management) to ensure operational safety and data integrity."
]
